title: Ayushman Bharat (PMJAY)
meta_description: Ayushman Bharat PMJAY provides ₹5 lakh health insurance per family per year. Check eligibility, hospital list, get your Ayushman card.

hero.one_liner: ₹5 lakh free health insurance coverage per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization

what_is_it: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme, launched on 23 September 2018. It provides health coverage of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. The scheme covers over 1,900 medical procedures including surgeries, treatments, and diagnostics.

The scheme is completely cashless and paperless at the point of care. You show your Ayushman card at any empaneled hospital, get treated, and the hospital bills the government directly. There are no premiums to pay — the government bears the entire cost. The scheme targets the bottom 40% of India's population based on SECC (Socio-Economic Caste Census) 2011 data.

eligibility.label: Who is eligible
eligibility.value: Families identified in SECC 2011 database (bottom 40% economically)

eligibility.label: Coverage
eligibility.value: ₹5 lakh per family per year — no cap on family size

eligibility.label: Pre-existing diseases
eligibility.value: Covered from day one — no waiting period

eligibility.label: Age limit
eligibility.value: No age limit — covers all family members

eligibility.label: Documents needed
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card, ration card, or any valid government ID

eligibility.label: How to check
eligibility.value: Visit mera.pmjay.gov.in and enter mobile number or ration card number

how_to_apply.title: Check if you're eligible
how_to_apply.description: Visit mera.pmjay.gov.in → Enter your mobile number linked to ration card → If eligible, your name will appear in the beneficiary list.

how_to_apply.title: Get your Ayushman card
how_to_apply.description: Visit any CSC (Common Service Centre), empaneled hospital, or download via Ayushman App. Carry Aadhaar card and ration card. Card is generated free of cost.

how_to_apply.title: Visit any empaneled hospital when needed
how_to_apply.description: Search hospitals at hospitals.pmjay.gov.in. Show your Ayushman card at the hospital. Treatment is fully cashless — no money needed from your side.

important_alert.text: You don't need to apply separately. If your family is in the SECC 2011 list, you're automatically eligible. Just get your Ayushman card made at a CSC center. Check eligibility at mera.pmjay.gov.in first.

important_dates.label: Card generation
important_dates.value: Free, available anytime at CSC centers

faqs.question: How do I check if I'm eligible for Ayushman Bharat?
faqs.answer: Visit mera.pmjay.gov.in and enter your mobile number or ration card number. If your family was identified in SECC 2011 data, you'll see your name and can generate your Ayushman card.

faqs.question: Is there any premium or fee?
faqs.answer: No. The scheme is completely free for eligible families. No premium, no registration fee, no copayment. The entire cost is borne by central and state governments.

faqs.question: Can I use Ayushman card at private hospitals?
faqs.answer: Yes, at any private hospital that is empaneled under PMJAY. Check the list at hospitals.pmjay.gov.in. Over 28,000 hospitals (both government and private) are empaneled.

faqs.question: Are pre-existing diseases covered?
faqs.answer: Yes. All pre-existing diseases are covered from day one. There is no waiting period for any condition.

faqs.question: What if my name is not in the list but I'm poor?
faqs.answer: The eligibility is based on SECC 2011 data. If your name was missed, contact your nearest Ayushman Mitra or visit the District Implementation Unit. Some states have expanded the scheme to additional families.

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
